title: Prefix And Suffix Search
hints: Check if the problem allows preprocessing words once to speed up repeated queries, or if you must search from scratch each time.
Store words in two separate lookup structures: one indexed by prefix and another by suffix, mapping each to a list of candidate weights.

Because a word's weight is just its position, the highest-weight match is simply the latest qualifying word — you never compare weights, only find the last word satisfying both ends. Whatever indexing you build, lean on that 'latest one wins' property.
